label: Azure MySQL
description: Connect to an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instance using host, port, credentials, and SSL.

form.title: Connect Azure MySQL
form.subtitle: Enter your Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server connection details to get started.

methods.label: Host / Credentials
methods.description: Paste your server hostname, port, database name, and admin credentials. SSL is required by Azure and enabled automatically.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Host & Credentials

Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server requires SSL. Gather the details below from the Azure portal before filling in the form.

## Steps

1. Open the [Azure portal](https://portal.azure.com) and navigate to your **Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server** resource.
2. In the left menu, click **Overview**. Copy the **Server name** (e.g. `myserver.mysql.database.azure.com`) — paste it into **Host** below.
3. The default port is **3306** unless you changed it during provisioning.
4. Under **Settings → Databases**, find or create the database you want Anton to connect to.
5. Under **Settings → Server parameters**, confirm `require_secure_transport` is `ON` (it is by default). Anton will always connect over SSL.
6. Use the admin username you set when creating the server (format: `username` — Azure may display it as `username@servername` in older UIs, but Flexible Server accepts the short form).
7. If you forgot the password, go to **Settings → Reset password** to set a new one.
8. Fill in all fields below and click **Connect**.

## Notes

- Flexible Server blocks public access by default. Ensure your network has access via **Networking → Add current client IP** or a configured VNet rule.
- If your firewall blocks the connection, add Anton's outbound IP in **Settings → Networking → Firewall rules**.
- The `require_secure_transport` parameter cannot be disabled on Azure MySQL Flexible Server for security compliance.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: The fully-qualified server name from the Azure portal Overview page.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: MySQL port — default is 3306 for Azure Flexible Server.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the specific database to connect to on this server.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Your MySQL admin or app user. Azure Flexible Server accepts the short username without the @servername suffix.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the MySQL user. Reset via Azure portal → Settings → Reset password if needed.

fields.name: ssl_enabled
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: SSL is mandatory on Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server and is always enforced. Leave this on.
